storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
A silver tongue is the nectar exuded by budding flowers, and courtiers are the bees and butterflies that flit around their swaying petals.
Thorns born of desire have unknowingly entwined the flower-decorated window tightly.
Even if her character is condemned by the world and her hands bleed from the cuts of the crown, she will never relinquish the power she has embraced.
After all, this is the true nourishment that allows the rose to bloom.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
"The Salon" has quietly risen in the upper class of Londinium at an unknown time.
In this mysterious salon, some have had the chance to receive portraits painted by masters, some have dined with the Prime Minister, and others have even received a promise from the Queen herself.
The organizer, the venue, and the timing of the event remain a mystery. People only know that everyone can obtain what they desire most in the salon.
But no one knows what kind of guest will earn the host's favor and receive an invitation.
Even the eloquent Lord Dexter was turned away by the salon's master.
"I'm very sorry, but the host currently prefers to invite artistic luminaries."
"Nobles? Please await future invitations."

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
At first, it was just a glass of grape soda that replaced red wine.
In an unnoticed corner, Sorel appeared pitiful, drinking with two or three students.
Then, it was a small afternoon tea.
In the student cafeteria, Sorel hesitated over the macarons pushed towards her, revealing a hint of reluctance and sincerity.
Finally, it was a grand salon, the blooming of a rose.
The lounge was adorned with flowers and jewels, brilliant roses mingling with naive students, and everyone enjoyed themselves.
The newly appointed Keeper witnessed all this and silently prayed that her next step would not be "becoming the principal of Mythag University."

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Sorel knows she is beautiful, and in her heart, she regards this beauty as a gift from heaven.
As long as she pleads with tearful eyes, people will show her pity; by simply acting pitiful, those who admire her will unconditionally take her side.
However, beauty is worthless in the face of true power.
For the first time, facing parents who wish to trade her to nobility for their own future, Sorel's tears tasted the bitterness of failure.
Is there anyone who can help her? Anyone who can take her away from her ignorant and backward fate?
Is there something, somewhere, that can transform her beauty into a real weapon?
As Sorel sobs with her face buried in her hands, an agent of deep destiny arrives before her.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Even now, as an Awakener, Sorel cannot forget those nobles who fawned over her; she cannot forget their faces, one after another, etched with flattery.

How utterly nauseating those smiling faces were: slick, mercenary, every one of them oozing desire and calculation, yet she had no choice but to weave a gentle web around each one.

She would weave the most meticulous of webs, reaching into every dark corner, winning over every person, seizing every piece of leverage. She had to be like water slowly brought to a boil, letting those people sink deeper and deeper, so that by the time they wished to remove their masks, they would find there was no escape.

But when she finally came to her senses, the one with no escape turned out to be herself.

No escape. No rest. No surrender.

Sorel has long since lost the right to turn back. Aside from standing above everyone, there is no other path for her to walk.
